name: fixture-07-staff-software-engineer-fintech
description: Staff engineer at a payments startup applying to a Principal Engineer role at a neobank. IC-track candidate, deep distributed-systems background; stretch is regulated-bank operational environment.

resume_data.name: Aarav Patel
key_achievements: Designed and led the rewrite of the authorization path from monolith to an event-sourced platform — handled 2.4B transactions in the first year with 99.998% availability and p99 latency under 40ms.
Introduced SLO-based oncall governance across 8 teams — reduced page volume 71% quarter-over-quarter while tightening reliability targets.
Mentored 6 engineers to senior promotion over 3 years; three are now tech leads on platform-critical surfaces.

requirements: Deep expertise in high-throughput distributed systems — ideally in payments, ledger, or adjacent domains
Demonstrated architecture ownership at the principal / staff-plus level
Experience operating in a regulated environment — SOC 2, PCI DSS, or bank-grade controls
Strong written and verbal communication — this role writes RFCs read by regulators
Willing to be hands-on in code as well as in architecture
culture_cues: regulated bank — change review is rigorous
document-heavy engineering culture — written design lives forever
principal engineers set direction, not just react

letter_plan.opening_hook: Lead with the authorization-path rewrite — 2.4B transactions, 99.998% availability, p99 under 40ms — to establish the payments + distributed-systems depth before the regulated-bank step-up.
body_points: Event-sourced authorization rewrite is a direct analog to core-banking ledger work — same exactly-once semantics, same reconciliation discipline, same availability bar. Position this as the shape the Principal role will expand (from payments rail to full core banking).
SLO-based oncall governance across 8 teams demonstrates the org-level architecture ownership the JD flags — this is principal-level impact, not staff-level IC delivery.

Mentoring 6 engineers to senior promotion proves the direction-setting muscle the regulated-bank culture demands. Mention that the payments-startup background is already SOC 2 + PCI DSS; the bank-grade controls are an additive learning layer, not a standing start.
letter_plan.closing_strategy: Acknowledge the regulator-readable RFC craft honestly — payments-startup design docs skew internal-engineer-focused, so the regulator-readable writing register is something to adapt to. Close on the direction-setting mandate as the explicit reason for applying — not just bigger scale, but a role where principal engineers actually set direction.
